overview.location: Queens, New York — 16 miles southeast of Midtown Manhattan
overview.distinction: Busiest international commercial airport in North America
overview.operator: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

terminals.name: Terminal 1
terminals.status: Being replaced by New Terminal One (Phase 1 opening June 2026)
terminals.notes: 28+ carriers total. New Terminal One ($9.5B) will replace Terminals 1, 2, and 3 sites. Phase 1 opens June 2026 with 13 gates. Full 23-gate completion by 2030. 2.6M sq ft with 300,000+ sq ft of retail/dining/lounges.

terminals.name: Terminal 4
terminals.notes: 22 airlines, 25+ million passengers/year. Delta is the dominant carrier (~30% market share). Operated by JFKIAT.

terminals.name: Terminal 5
terminals.notes: JetBlue's flagship terminal. $100M+ refresh adding 40+ new concessions (Shake Shack, Eataly). BlueHouse premium lounge opened Dec 2025. Will connect seamlessly to new Terminal 6.

terminals.name: Terminal 6
terminals.status: NEW — Phase 1 opening first half of 2026
terminals.notes: Brand new $4.2B terminal, 10 gates optimized for widebody aircraft. Phase 1 (6 gates) opening 2026, full completion 2028. Connects to Terminal 5. Developed by JFK Millennium Partners. 15 airlines confirmed.

terminals.name: Terminal 7
terminals.status: Active but CLOSING — scheduled for demolition in 2026
terminals.notes: British Airways moved to Terminal 8 in Nov 2022. Terminal 7 will be demolished to make way for Terminal 6 Phase 2. Airlines will relocate to T6/T8. Managed by JMP until closure.

terminals.name: Terminal 8
terminals.notes: American Airlines hub. British Airways relocated here from T7 in late 2022. 10 carriers.

terminalChanges2026: Around 50 airlines (~2/3 of all JFK carriers) are expected to change terminals in 2026 due to the opening of New Terminal One and Terminal 6, plus the demolition of Terminal 7. Travelers should verify their terminal before every trip.
authority: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

summerDiscount.reason: 50% reduction to encourage public transit during peak construction

freeStations: All terminal stations (inter-terminal travel is free)

subway: Z lines at Sutphin Blvd-Archer Ave station
jamaica.lirr: 20 minutes to Penn Station/Grand Central Madison/Atlantic Terminal

subway: A line at Howard Beach station

airTrain.competitorNote: CRITICAL for off-site parking comparison: AirTrain costs $8.50/person one-way ($17.00 round-trip). A couple pays $34 round-trip on AirTrain alone. Off-site lots with direct shuttle service eliminate this cost entirely.

taxi.notes: Metered to other destinations. Flat rate is predictable — no surge pricing.

rideshare.surgeFrequency: 34% of Manhattan-bound rides experience surge pricing
rideshare.notes: No flat rate — upfront pricing shown. Can be cheaper off-peak but unpredictable during rush hours.

parkingRates: On-airport: Yellow/Blue Garage $6/half-hr ($80/day max, $48 pre-booked), Red Garage (T8) $4/half-hr ($70/day max, $36 pre-booked), Economy Lot 9 $29/first 24hrs then $10/hr. Off-site via Triply: $10.62-$30.45/day. IMPORTANT: AirTrain costs $8.50/person one-way — off-site lots with direct shuttles save this cost vs. on-airport parking + AirTrain from Lot 9.

lot9Economy.connection: Free AirTrain from Lefferts Blvd station to all terminals
lot9Economy.notes: Cheapest on-airport option but still requires AirTrain transfer (free from Lot 9 station)

cellPhoneLot.rules: Driver must remain with vehicle at all times

federalCircle.description: AirTrain station for hotel shuttles, rental car offices, and off-site parking lot shuttles
federalCircle.airTrainCost: Free (no fare charged at Federal Circle)

shuttleInfo: Most off-site lots run free shuttles every 10-45 minutes. AirTrain connects all terminals (free for inter-terminal travel). Federal Circle AirTrain station is free and serves as the pickup point for many hotel/parking shuttles. Some budget lots (Parking 4 Airport, Hyatt Place Flushing, Queens Crossing) have limited shuttle hours with NO overnight service.
distanceFromCity: 16 miles southeast of Midtown Manhattan, roughly 45-75 min by car depending on traffic. 20 min via LIRR to Penn Station + AirTrain.

construction2026.status: Major $19 billion redevelopment at peak activity in 2026
projects: New Terminal One ($9.5B) — Phase 1 opening June 2026, 13 gates, full completion 2030
New Terminal 6 ($4.2B) — Phase 1 (6 gates) opening first half 2026, full completion 2028
Terminal 7 demolition scheduled for 2026 to make room for T6 Phase 2
Terminal 5 refresh by JetBlue — 40+ new concessions, completion end of 2026
Major roadway reconstruction causing significant traffic delays
construction2026.travelImpact: Airlines recommend arriving 2-3 hours before departure. Multiple parking lots advise arriving 3 hours early. Roadway construction causing delays getting into airport. About 50 airlines changing terminals in 2026.
construction2026.airTrainDiscount: 50% off AirTrain fare ($4.25 instead of $8.50) from June 30 through Labor Day to reduce road congestion during peak construction

parkingLots.name: Safe Park (JFK)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: 24/7, every 45 minutes. Valet service — must leave keys.
parkingLots.overview: Located just 5 minutes drive from JFK Airport, right next to the JFK Expressway. Restrooms on premises. Valet team parks your car.
knownCoupons: Up to 20% off via coupon aggregators
parkingLots.notes: JFK construction may affect shuttle times. Every 7th day free on their direct website at $24.95/day.

parkingLots.name: Purchase Park 2 Fly (JFK)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: 24/7, on demand. Call 888-960-7275 for return shuttle pickup.
parkingLots.overview: Professional valet service with complimentary luggage assistance. Fully fenced and staffed 24/7 with video surveillance. GPS address: 123-70 S Conduit Ave (some GPS may need 124-10 S Conduit Ave). Look for blue and orange signage.
parkingLots.notes: Third driveway on the right when traveling on South Conduit Avenue. Must leave keys.

parkingLots.name: DoubleTree by Hilton (JFK)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: 24/7, every 30 minutes (first-come, first-served). Return pickup: call 718-322-2300. Pickup at Federal Circle — white van with DoubleTree signage.
parkingLots.overview: Formerly Radisson JFK. Fully gated, monitored 24/7 by on-site staff and video surveillance. Hotel amenities accessible. Arrive 30 min before desired shuttle departure.
parkingLots.notes: No taxes on Triply rate. Renovation traffic warning — arrive at least 3 hours before flight. Must leave keys.

parkingLots.name: Bolt Parking (JFK)
parkingLots.overview: Convenient valet parking with 24/7 security and attentive staff. Located 5 minutes from JFK.
parkingLots.notes: Direct website rate starts at $18.99/day. Must leave keys.

parkingLots.name: ARB Parking (JFK)
parkingLots.overview: Budget-friendly off-airport parking. Fast shuttle to all JFK terminals. 24/7 staff, fenced and gated lot. Arrive 30 min before desired shuttle departure.
parkingLots.notes: Surcharge for SUVs: small SUV $5/day extra, large SUV/truck $7/day extra. Available on Groupon. Direct rate from $12.95/day.

parkingLots.name: PARK AC JFK Airport Parking (JFK)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: 24/7, every 10 minutes to and from all JFK terminals.
parkingLots.overview: Cheapest JFK-dedicated lot on Triply. Entrance at corner of 126th Street and 152nd Avenue. Monitored around the clock. Self-park with staff directing you.
parkingLots.notes: Lowest daily rate for JFK-proximity lots. JFK renovation may cause traffic delays — arrive 3 hours before flight. Mixed reviews on pickup wait times.

parkingLots.name: Parking 4 Airport (JFK)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: 5:15 AM - 9:00 PM only, every 20-30 minutes. NO shuttle 9 PM - 5:15 AM (use Uber/Lyft). Return: call from Arrivals pickup area.
parkingLots.overview: Affordable option but farther from JFK (15-min drive). Limited shuttle hours. Valet with key drop required.
parkingLots.notes: NO overnight shuttle — must use rideshare after 9 PM. Arrive 30-60 min before desired airport time. Farther away than other options.

parkingLots.name: Hyatt Place Flushing - LAZ Parking (LGA)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: 7 AM - 10 PM, every hour on the hour. NO shuttle 10 PM - 7 AM. Call 718-888-1234 press 0 for return pickup.
parkingLots.overview: Indoor garage at Hyatt Place hotel in Flushing. Elevator from lot to hotel lobby with coffee-to-cocktails bar, free wifi, rooftop lounge (5PM-1AM). Security cameras. Does NOT accept pickup trucks, extra-large trucks, vans, or oversized vehicles.
parkingLots.notes: Technically an LGA lot but appears in JFK search results. Covered/indoor parking. No oversized vehicles. Arrive 2 hours before flight. Limited shuttle hours.

parkingLots.name: Queens Crossing - LAZ Parking (LGA)
parkingLots.shuttleInfo: 6:30 AM - 10:30 PM, every hour on the half-hour. NO shuttle 10:30 PM - 6:30 AM. Call 718-888-1234 press 0 for return.
parkingLots.overview: Budget indoor garage parking near Flushing. Prepaid only — must print receipt and present to valet. 7'6" clearance. Open weekdays 6 AM - 11:59 PM, weekends 7 AM - 11:59 PM.
parkingLots.notes: Cheapest option but 8.7 mi from JFK. LGA lot appearing in JFK results. Limited availability badge shown. Must print receipt — no receipt = no service, no refund. Arrive 2 hours before flight. Limited shuttle hours.

tips: Subscribe to JFK Long Term Parking email newsletter for up to 30% off exclusive deals
AAA members can get up to 20% off at participating JFK lots
Check Groupon for bundled deals on ARB Parking and PARK AC
Book through aggregators like Triply, Way.com, or SpotHero to compare rates
Prices are dynamic — booking further in advance generally locks in lower rates
AirTrain is 50% off ($4.25) during summer (June 30 - Labor Day) due to construction

evCharging.terminal5: 5 dual-plug ChargePoint stations (10 plugs total) in Yellow Parking Level 2. Requires ChargePoint RFID card.
evCharging.fhvHoldLot: 24 brand-new EV chargers in the for-hire vehicle hold lot (accessible via Van Wyck Expressway or Belt Parkway).
evCharging.nearbyMegaHub: Wildflower EV station (largest in NYC) breaking ground 2026 north of JFK between Nassau Expressway and Rockaway Boulevard — 65+ charging stations including 12 rapid chargers, 24/7.
evCharging.offSiteLots: No off-site parking lots currently advertise EV charging. EV drivers should charge before parking.

standardWaitTimes.terminal5Note: Most clear within 10 minutes, but spikes around 9 AM

terminal1: Air France Lounge (9:45AM-11:30PM, near Air France boarding area Gate 1)
terminal4: Air India Maharaja Lounge (Priority Pass accepted, subject to capacity)
terminal5: Be Relax Spa (opposite Gates 6-7, Priority Pass: select complimentary treatments)
terminal8: Bobby Van's Steakhouse (opposite Gate 14, 6AM-9PM, Priority Pass: $28 credit)
lounges.priorityPassTip: Priority Pass holders get $28 off at Bobby Van's Steakhouse (T8) and select treatments at Be Relax Spa (T5). HelloSky and Air India Maharaja lounges in T4 accept Priority Pass subject to capacity.

wifi.cost: Free — no registration, no time limit
wifi.note: 15-20% of users may experience issues during peak travel. Lounge wifi is significantly faster and more secure.

luggageStorage.note: No luggage lockers inside JFK (removed for security). Only staffed counters at T1 and T4.

terminals.terminal1: 1 area — outside Arrivals, east end, pre-security
terminals.terminal4: 4 areas — indoor on Level 2 near gates, indoor post-security near Gate B31 Level 3, outdoor Level 1 east end, outdoor curbside Level 1 west end
terminals.terminal5: 2 areas — Wooftop outdoor rooftop near Gate 28 Level 3 (largest at JFK, post-security), outdoor curbside Level 1 north end pre-security
terminals.terminal7: 2 areas — outdoor curbside Level 1, indoor post-security Level 3 near Gates 9-10
terminals.terminal8: 2 areas — indoor post-security near Gate 38 Level 3

arkFacility.name: The ARK at JFK
arkFacility.location: Cargo Area D, off the runway
arkFacility.animals: Horses, pets, companion animals, birds, livestock
arkFacility.note: One of the most unique animal facilities at any airport worldwide

peakTravelTimes.bestTimeToFly: Early morning (6-8 AM) — 80%+ on-time arrival rate

arrivalRecommendation.during2026Construction: Add 30-60 extra minutes due to roadway construction and terminal changes

manhattanToJFK.freeBridges: Most other bridge crossings are toll-free

fromLongIsland.route: Southern State Parkway to Belt Parkway West Exit 20

tolls.taxiTollsIncluded: Tolls ($6-$9.50) are added on top of the $70 flat fare to Manhattan

lot9Economy.airTrainNote: Free from Lot 9 Lefferts Blvd station

parkAC.airTrainNote: Direct shuttle to terminal — no AirTrain needed

arbParking.airTrainNote: Direct shuttle every 10-15 min — no AirTrain needed

rentalCars.location: Federal Circle — accessible via free AirTrain
rentalCars.note: Must take AirTrain to Federal Circle station (free). AirTrain runs every 2-5 min.

parkSleepFly.description: Hotel + parking + shuttle packages near JFK — stay the night before, park free for your trip, shuttle to terminal.
popularHotels: DoubleTree by Hilton JFK (24/7 shuttle every 30 min)
Holiday Inn Express JFK (free breakfast + shuttle + wifi)

locations.terminal4: Arrivals Hall, Concourse B Gate 31, Retail Hall East, Retail Hall West
locations.terminal5: Near food court (close to iS Duty Free)

currencyExchange.atms: Available in every terminal, 24/7, dispense USD only. Accept international cards. Fee: up to $5/transaction for non-network users.
currencyExchange.warning: Airport exchange rates charge up to 14% markup vs. outside rates. Use ATM or exchange before arriving for better rates.

interTerminalTransfers.criticalNote: All international arrivals must clear Customs, collect checked bags, and re-check them — even for connecting flights. You must exit security and re-clear at your departure terminal.
interTerminalTransfers.during2026Construction: Add extra time — terminal changes affecting ~50 airlines. Verify your terminal before every trip.

weatherPatterns.commonDisruptions: Snow, ice, nor'easters, summer thunderstorms, fog
weatherPatterns.winterImpact: Major storms can cancel 400+ flights. JFK's location (surrounded by water and dense development) limits runway flexibility during severe weather.
weatherPatterns.cascadingEffect: JFK disruptions cascade nationwide — 5,000+ flights affected during major storms as inbound flights are preemptively canceled.
weatherPatterns.summerStorms: Afternoon thunderstorms June-August can cause 1-3 hour delays
weatherPatterns.bestMonths: September-October and April-May typically see fewest weather disruptions

locations.terminal1: Gate 1 (outside Air France Lounge), Gate C70
locations.terminal4: Gate A3, Gate B31, West Retail Hall, Level 3 private family room with changing table

lactationRooms.features: Lockable door, electrical outlets, seating for family of 4 + luggage

blade.luggage: Under 25lbs included. Extra bag: $145 advance / $175 at lounge
blade.heliports: West 30th St and East 34th St Manhattan
blade.includes: Ground transportation between helicopter and flight
blade.note: Joby Aviation (electric air taxi) acquired BLADE's passenger service. Bookable through Uber app starting 2026.

liveSources._description: Authoritative URLs to scrape for fresh data when generating articles. Grouped by topic.
